prompt: Explain how a route defined with the Java DSL actually gets started in this codebase: walk through what happens between RouteBuilder.configure() being called and the route consuming its first exchange.

Explain how the type converter system resolves a conversion between two types at runtime in this codebase: where do converters come from, and how is one looked up and applied when an exchange body needs converting?
Explain how the SEDA component hands an exchange from producer to consumer in this codebase: what role does the underlying queue play, and what happens when the queue is full?

Explain the error-handling flow in this codebase: when an exception is thrown while an exchange is being processed mid-route, how does the exchange end up at a dead letter endpoint, and what decides whether it is redelivered first?

Explain how content-based routing works in this codebase: when a route uses choice/when/otherwise, how are the predicates evaluated against an exchange at runtime and how is the winning branch selected?
Explain what happens when the core context that owns routes is started in this codebase: what gets initialized, in what order, before any route begins consuming?
